substrate: frontend/src/lib/format.ts (pure display formatters, zero runtime imports) + backend/src/services/search.services.ts (keyword search; pure helpers driven through the exported searchRecordings in keyword mode). TypeScript / bun:test; no third-party runtime deps; pure, offline-isolable.
base_image: meetnotes-repo:v1 = oven/bun:1 + git + python3; flattened clean workspace copied to /app; NO bun install (no runtime deps).
task_image: meetnotes-task:v1 = FROM meetnotes-repo:v1 + 5 single-token byte-substring defects + fresh 1-commit git history + /logs/verifier.
difficulty_mix: 2 EASY (megabyte precision constant, index<0 vs <=0 guard) + 3 MEDIUM (hour-cutoff comparison flip, dropped case-normalization, off-by-one result cap)
distinct_shapes: numeric precision constant (toFixed(1) -> toFixed(0)) on one branch, sibling-pinned
off-by-one on a result-count bound (slice(0,limit) -> slice(0,limit+1)), sibling-pinned
grep_twin_note: Two of the five are comparison flips, but on unrelated invariants in different files/functions (formatDuration hour arithmetic vs getTextPreview indexOf contract), on different operators (>= vs <), each pinned by a different sibling. No single grep surfaces both, and fixing one does not reveal the other. The other three shapes are distinct mechanisms.

defects.edge_input: a duration of exactly 60 minutes, e.g. formatDuration(3600) or formatDuration(3659)
defects.wrong_output: '60:00' / '60:59' (minutes-and-seconds form with an oversized minute count) instead of '1h 0m'
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: sub-hour durations and 61-minutes-and-up render identically under >= and >; only the exact 60-minute band (3600-3659 s) diverges, which no mid-range value hits.
defects.derivability: The hour branch immediately below computes hours = floor(minutes/60) and remMin = minutes % 60; at exactly 60 minutes that branch yields the consistent '1h 0m'. The cutoff must include 60 for the hours form to own the 60-minute point, exactly as the sibling arithmetic implies.

defects.edge_input: a megabyte-scale size, e.g. formatBytes(5*1024*1024) or formatBytes(1_572_864)
defects.wrong_output: '5 MB' / '2 MB' (no decimal; 1.5 rounded to a whole number) instead of '5.0 MB' / '1.5 MB'
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: byte (< 1 KB) and kilobyte (< 1 MB) sizes never reach the MB branch; the KB branch's own toFixed(1) is untouched.
defects.derivability: The sibling KB branch one line up formats with .toFixed(1); the size column is meant to carry one decimal across units, so the MB branch must match its sibling's precision.

defects.edge_input: a lowercase query against stored text of different case, e.g. searchRecordings keyword mode q='launch' over a title 'Quarterly Launch Plan' / transcript 'Meeting about the LAUNCH schedule'
defects.wrong_output: no match (recording dropped from keywordResults) instead of a hit with matchedFields ['title','transcript']
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: same-case matches still succeed; only a case-mismatched query/stored-text pair diverges, which no comfortable-case value feeds. The visible repo suite only exercises semantic mode, so the keyword path is untested there.
defects.derivability: The query is lowercased before it arrives (normalizedQuery = query.toLowerCase() in searchRecordingsByKeyword); matching only one side case-folds a comparison that is meant to be case-insensitive on both. The mirror getTextPreview also lowercases the text before indexOf.

defects.boundary_changed: return results.slice(0, limit)  ->  return results.slice(0, limit + 1)
defects.edge_input: more matches than the limit, e.g. 3 matching recordings with limit = 2
defects.wrong_output: 3 results returned instead of 2 (one past the requested cap)
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: result sets at or under the limit are unaffected by the +1; only an over-limit match set diverges.
defects.derivability: The sibling semantic branch caps its results with the identical .slice(0, limit); the keyword branch must honour the same limit contract its caller passes.

defects.edge_input: a query at position 0 of a long transcript, e.g. a transcript beginning with the query and longer than 180 chars
defects.wrong_output: the no-match fallback head slice (first 180 chars) instead of the leading match window (first 120 chars)
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: a mid-transcript match (index > 0) is identical under < 0 and <= 0; only a match at the exact start (index 0), on a transcript long enough for 120 vs 180 to differ, diverges.
defects.derivability: String.indexOf returns -1 only when absent and a valid found position is >= 0, so 0 is a real match; the guard must exclude only -1, exactly as indexOf's contract implies.

f2p: tests/lib_boundaries.test.ts::audio duration formatting::rolls a full hour of seconds into hours-and-minutes form
tests/lib_boundaries.test.ts::size formatting::keeps one decimal place for megabyte sizes
tests/lib_boundaries.test.ts::keyword search matching::matches a query against stored text regardless of letter case
tests/lib_boundaries.test.ts::keyword search result count::returns no more results than the requested limit
tests/lib_boundaries.test.ts::keyword search preview::windows the preview around a match at the start of the transcript
p2p: tests/lib_boundaries.test.ts::audio duration formatting::formats sub-hour durations as minutes and seconds
tests/lib_boundaries.test.ts::audio duration formatting::keeps minutes-and-seconds form just below the hour
tests/lib_boundaries.test.ts::audio duration formatting::keeps hours-and-minutes form above the hour boundary
tests/lib_boundaries.test.ts::size formatting::formats byte and kilobyte sizes
tests/lib_boundaries.test.ts::size formatting::uses plain bytes below one kilobyte
tests/lib_boundaries.test.ts::size formatting::stays in kilobytes just below one megabyte
tests/lib_boundaries.test.ts::keyword search matching::matches a lowercase query within a same-case title
tests/lib_boundaries.test.ts::keyword search matching::returns nothing when the query is absent
tests/lib_boundaries.test.ts::keyword search result count::returns every match when the match count is under the limit
tests/lib_boundaries.test.ts::keyword search preview::returns the surrounding text for a mid-transcript match
tests/lib_boundaries.test.ts::keyword search preview::previews the title when the transcript has no text
tests/lib_boundaries.test.ts::search modes::returns empty result sets for semantic mode with no vector hits
base_defects_visible_suite: N/A — the minimal offline image ships no agent-visible bun test files (the repo's own search test is vitest-based and needs the full backend + Firebase/Qdrant deps, which are out of the offline slice). The agent-visible tree is source only; there is no red test at the planted state. All f2p+p2p live in the hidden gold file injected at grade time.

ladder.leak_check: PASS — in meetnotes-task:v1 (--network none): git log length 1, git status --porcelain empty, git diff empty, planted tokens present, no tests/ dir (gold file absent from the agent image).
ladder.null_run: reward 0 — full test.sh on the unmodified task image: all 5 fail_to_pass report per-test FAILED (12/17 required passed); 17 tests parsed, no collection errors.
ladder.oracle_run: reward 1 — solution/solve.sh reverse-applies the 5 replacements; 17/17 required passed (all passed).
ladder.partial_run: reward 0 — fixing only the 2 format.ts defects leaves the 3 search.services.ts f2p failing (14/17 required passed).

litmus_result: FAIR — the instruction names the two source files but no function, value, count, boundary direction, or defect shape; it reports user-visible wrong behaviour at the edges. With no failing local test there is no red-test gradient, so a strong dev must read the boundary logic in both files, locate five slips among many boundaries (two of them in one file), and derive each correct side from a sibling. Naming the files (but not lines/functions/values/counts) matches the round-1 calibration where module-level naming without contract-level detail gated well.
fairness_audit: Every f2p asserts input/output behaviour on exported surfaces (formatDuration, formatBytes, and the exported searchRecordings entrypoint in keyword mode), never an implementation detail. Each defect's correct side is derivable from a sibling in the same file: the hour arithmetic below the cutoff, the KB branch's toFixed(1), the pre-lowercased query, the semantic branch's identical slice(0,limit), and indexOf's found-position contract. The 12 controls block lazy over-fixes (sub-hour and above-hour durations, byte/KB sizes, same-case and absent matches, under-limit counts, mid-transcript and title-fallback previews, empty semantic results). The only collaborator on the search path is the recordings data source, supplied as a typed offline double via __setRecordings with explicit values (no bare mock, no module-attribute patching); it is never the object under assertion, and a near-correct implementation reading its legitimate fields cannot crash on a mock. Alternative-implementation audit: each defect has a materially different correct fix (manual case-fold, Math.min-based cap, explicit found-index check, per-branch toFixed) that passes both its f2p and the controls; no control pins the internal split of the 180-char preview window (only the leading-window length the guard alone determines). Deterministic, fully offline, verifier runs --network none.
harness_notes: TS/bun adaptation of the canonical SWE-Bench-Pro contract, copied from the proven snapback harness. run_script.sh runs `bun test tests/lib_boundaries.test.ts --reporter=junit`; parser.py is the regex-based JUnit-XML parser (no pyexpat) emitting names as '<file>::<describe>::<it>'; test.sh is the canonical entrypoint (applies test_patch from config, runs, parses, requires all f2p+p2p passed). Defects are planted and reversed by exact single-token byte-substring replacement (CRLF-safe: the two source files ship with CRLF line endings, which a context-diff patch would fight; each target string occurs exactly once, verified, or the plant/solve aborts). The search service's four collaborator imports are satisfied by tiny offline test doubles in the image so searchRecordings resolves and runs with no network.
